methods.name: Peer-Median

data.narrative:
MakinaRocks is a Series B-stage AI & Software company that has raised $530 million to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of five same-stage, same-sector private peers (Safe Superintelligence, Physical Intelligence, World Labs, Nominal, and Photoroom), with valuations ranging from $500 million to $32.0 billion, alongside one public software comparator (PLTR).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy for entry valuation, applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $1.0 billion. Peer-median analysis across the five comparable companies produces a median valuation of $5.0 billion, with a range spanning $500 million to $32.0 billion. The wide peer dispersion reflects pronounced heterogeneity in market positioning and capital efficiency within early-stage frontier AI.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $856 million (low) to $5.8 billion (high), with a median estimate of $2.5 billion. This range reflects material uncertainty driven by two factors: the high variance in peer valuations, particularly the $32.0 billion outlier (Safe Superintelligence), and the limited visibility into MakinaRocks' revenue, unit economics, and technological differentiation relative to the peer set. Additional clarity on those operational metrics would materially narrow the valuation band.
